NKJV.1: Now when the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, that they might come and anoint Him.
NKJV.2: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.
NKJV.3: And they said among themselves, “Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?”
NKJV.4: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away—for it was very large.
NKJV.5: And entering the tomb, they saw a young man clothed in a long white robe sitting on the right side; and they were alarmed.
NKJV.6: But he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He is risen! He is not here. See the place where they laid Him.
NKJV.7: But go, tell His disciples—and Peter—that He is going before you into Galilee; there you will see Him, as He said to you.”
NKJV.8: So they went out quickly and fled from the tomb, for they trembled and were amazed. And they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
NKJV.9: Now when He rose early on the first day of the week, He appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom He had cast seven demons.
NKJV.10: She went and told those who had been with Him, as they mourned and wept.
NKJV.11: And when they heard that He was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe.
NKJV.12: After that, He appeared in another form to two of them as they walked and went into the country.
NKJV.13: And they went and told it to the rest, but they did not believe them either.
NKJV.14: Later He appeared to the eleven as they sat at the table; and He rebuked their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who had seen Him after He had risen.
NKJV.15: And He said to them, “Go into all the world  and preach the gospel to every creature.
NKJV.16: He who believes and is baptized will be saved;  but he who does not believe will be condemned.
NKJV.17: And these  signs will follow those who believe:  In My name they will cast out demons;  they will speak with new tongues;
NKJV.18: they will take up serpents; and if they drink anything deadly, it will by no means hurt them;  they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
NKJV.19: So then, after the Lord had spoken to them, He was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.
NKJV.20: And they went out and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them and confirming the word through the accompanying signs. Amen.

ESV.1: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him.
ESV.2: And very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb.
ESV.3: And they were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance of the tomb?”
ESV.4: And looking up, they saw that the stone had been rolled back — it was very large.
ESV.5: And entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were alarmed.
ESV.6: And he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You seek Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has risen; he is not here. See the place where they laid him.
ESV.7: But go, tell his disciples and Peter that he is going before you to Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.”
ESV.8: And they went out and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had seized them, and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
ESV.9: [Some of the earliest manuscripts do not include 16:9–20.] Jesus Appears to Mary Magdalene [[Now when he rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.
ESV.10: She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
ESV.11: But when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they would not believe it.
ESV.12: After these things he appeared in another form to two of them, as they were walking into the country.
ESV.13: And they went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them.
ESV.14: Afterward he appeared to the eleven themselves as they were reclining at table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who saw him after he had risen.
ESV.15: And he said to them,  “Go into all the world and proclaim the gospel to the whole creation.
ESV.16: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
ESV.17: And these signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
ESV.18: they will pick up serpents with their hands; and if they drink any deadly poison, it will not hurt them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
ESV.19: So then the Lord Jesus, after he had spoken to them, was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
ESV.20: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the message by accompanying signs.]]

NASB.1: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the [mother] of James, and Salome, bought spices, so that they might come and anoint Him.
NASB.2: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.
NASB.3: They were saying to one another, 'Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance of the tomb?'
NASB.4: Looking up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away, although it was extremely large.
NASB.5: Entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting at the right, wearing a white robe; and they were amazed.
NASB.6: And he said to them, 'Do not be amazed; you are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen; He is not here; behold, [here is] the place where they laid Him.
NASB.7: 'But go, tell His disciples and Peter, 'He is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see Him, just as He told you.''
NASB.8: They went out and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had gripped them; and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid.
NASB.9: [Now after He had risen early on the first day of the week, He first appeared to Mary Magdalene, from whom He had cast out seven demons.
NASB.10: She went and reported to those who had been with Him, while they were mourning and weeping.
NASB.11: When they heard that He was alive and had been seen by her, they refused to believe it.
NASB.12: After that, He appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking along on their way to the country.
NASB.13: They went away and reported it to the others, but they did not believe them either.
NASB.14: Afterward He appeared to the eleven themselves as they were reclining [at the table]; and He reproached them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who had seen Him after He had risen.
NASB.15: And He said to them, 'Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation.
NASB.16: 'He who has believed and has been baptized shall be saved; but he who has disbelieved shall be condemned.
NASB.17: 'These signs will accompany those who have believed: in My name they will cast out demons, they will speak with new tongues;
NASB.18: they will pick up serpents, and if they drink any deadly [poison], it will not hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.'
NASB.19: So then, when the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, He was received up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
NASB.20: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them, and confirmed the word by the signs that followed]. [[And they promptly reported all these instructions to Peter and his companions. And after that, Jesus Himself sent out through them from east to west the sacred and imperishable proclamation of eternal salvation].]

NLT.1: Saturday evening, when the Sabbath ended, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome went out and purchased burial spices so they could anoint Jesus’ body.
NLT.2: Very early on Sunday morning, just at sunrise, they went to the tomb.
NLT.3: On the way they were asking each other, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?”
NLT.4: But as they arrived, they looked up and saw that the stone, which was very large, had already been rolled aside.
NLT.5: When they entered the tomb, they saw a young man clothed in a white robe sitting on the right side. The women were shocked,
NLT.6: but the angel said, “Don’t be alarmed. You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He isn’t here! He is risen from the dead! Look, this is where they laid his body.
NLT.7: Now go and tell his disciples, including Peter, that Jesus is going ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you before he died.”
NLT.8: The women fled from the tomb, trembling and bewildered, and they said nothing to anyone because they were too frightened. [The most ancient manuscripts of Mark conclude with verse 16:8. Later manuscripts add one or both of the following endings.] [Shorter Ending of Mark] Then they briefly reported all this to Peter and his companions. Afterward Jesus himself sent them out from east to west with the sacred and unfailing message of salvation that gives eternal life. Amen. [Longer Ending of Mark]
NLT.9: After Jesus rose from the dead early on Sunday morning, the first person who saw him was Mary Magdalene, the woman from whom he had cast out seven demons.
NLT.10: She went to the disciples, who were grieving and weeping, and told them what had happened.
NLT.11: But when she told them that Jesus was alive and she had seen him, they didn’t believe her.
NLT.12: Afterward he appeared in a different form to two of his followers who were walking from Jerusalem into the country.
NLT.13: They rushed back to tell the others, but no one believed them.
NLT.14: Still later he appeared to the eleven disciples as they were eating together. He rebuked them for their stubborn unbelief because they refused to believe those who had seen him after he had been raised from the dead.
NLT.15: And then he told them, “Go into all the world and preach the Good News to everyone.
NLT.16: Anyone who believes and is baptized will be saved. But anyone who refuses to believe will be condemned.
NLT.17: These miraculous signs will accompany those who believe: They will cast out demons in my name, and they will speak in new languages.
NLT.18: They will be able to handle snakes with safety, and if they drink anything poisonous, it won’t hurt them. They will be able to place their hands on the sick, and they will be healed.”
NLT.19: When the Lord Jesus had finished talking with them, he was taken up into heaven and sat down in the place of honor at God’s right hand.
NLT.20: And the disciples went everywhere and preached, and the Lord worked through them, confirming what they said by many miraculous signs.

WEB.1: When the Sabbath was past, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome, bought spices, that they might come and anoint him.
WEB.2: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.
WEB.3: They were saying among themselves, “Who will roll away the stone from the door of the tomb for us?”
WEB.4: for it was very big. Looking up, they saw that the stone was rolled back.
WEB.5: Entering into the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed in a white robe, and they were amazed.
WEB.6: He said to them, “Don’t be amazed. You seek Jesus, the Nazarene, who has been crucified. He has risen. He is not here. Behold, the place where they laid him!
WEB.7: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He goes before you into Galilee. There you will see him, as he said to you.’”
WEB.8: They went out,  and fled from the tomb, for trembling and astonishment had come on them. They said nothing to anyone; for they were afraid.
WEB.9: Now when he had risen early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.
WEB.10: She went and told those who had been with him, as they mourned and wept.
WEB.11: When they heard that he was alive, and had been seen by her, they disbelieved.
WEB.12: After these things he was revealed in another form to two of them, as they walked, on their way into the country.
WEB.13: They went away and told it to the rest. They didn’t believe them, either.
WEB.14: Afterward he was revealed to the eleven themselves as they sat at the table, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they didn’t believe those who had seen him after he had risen.
WEB.15: He said to them, “Go into all the world, and preach the Good News to the whole creation.
WEB.16: He who believes and is baptized will be saved; but he who disbelieves will be condemned.
WEB.17: These signs will accompany those who believe: in my name they will cast out demons; they will speak with new languages;
WEB.18: they will take up serpents; and if they drink any deadly thing, it will in no way hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
WEB.19: So then the Lord,  after he had spoken to them, was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.
WEB.20: They went out, and preached everywhere, the Lord working with them, and confirming the word by the signs that followed. Amen.

CSB17.1: When <sup>ⓕ</sup> the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, <sup>ⓖ</sup> Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they could go and anoint him.
CSB17.2: Very early in the morning, on the first day of the week, <sup>ⓗ</sup> they went to the tomb <sup>ⓘ</sup> at sunrise.
CSB17.3: They were saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone from the entrance to the tomb for us?”
CSB17.4: Looking up, they noticed that the stone — which was very large — had been rolled away.
CSB17.5: When <sup>ⓙ</sup> they entered the tomb, <sup>ⓚ</sup> they saw a young man <sup>ⓛ</sup> dressed <sup>ⓜ</sup> in a white <sup>ⓝ</sup> robe sitting on the right side; they were alarmed.
CSB17.6: “Don’t be alarmed,” he told them. “You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, <sup>ⓐ</sup> who was crucified. He has risen! <sup>ⓑ</sup> He is not here. See the place where they put him.
CSB17.7: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, <sup>ⓒ</sup> ‘He is going ahead of you to Galilee; <sup>ⓓ</sup> you will see him there just as he told you.’”
CSB17.8: They went out and ran from the tomb, <sup>ⓔ</sup> because trembling and astonishment overwhelmed them. And they said nothing to anyone, <sup>ⓕ</sup> since they were afraid. [Some of the earliest mss conclude with 16:8.] <sup>[1]</sup> [
CSB17.9: Early <sup>ⓖ</sup> on the first day of the week, <sup>ⓗ</sup> after he had risen, <sup>ⓘ</sup> he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, <sup>ⓙ</sup> out of whom he had driven seven demons. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.10: She went and reported to those who had been with him, as they were mourning and weeping.
CSB17.11: Yet, when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe it. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.12: After this, he appeared <sup>ⓜ</sup> in a different form to two of them walking on their way into the country. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.13: And they went and reported it to the rest, who did not believe them either.
CSB17.14: Later he appeared to the Eleven <sup>ⓐ</sup> themselves as they were reclining at the table. He rebuked their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who saw him after he had risen. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.15: Then <sup>ⓒ</sup> he said to them, “Go into all the world <sup>ⓓ</sup> and preach the gospel to all creation. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.16: Whoever believes <sup>ⓕ</sup> and is baptized will be saved, <sup>ⓖ</sup> but whoever does not believe will be condemned. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.17: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues; <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.18: they will pick up snakes;  <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup>if they should drink anything deadly, it will not harm <sup>ⓙ</sup>them; they will lay hands on <sup>ⓚ</sup>the sick, and they will get well.”
CSB17.19: So the Lord Jesus, after speaking to them, <sup>ⓛ</sup> was taken up into heaven <sup>ⓜ</sup> and sat down at the right hand <sup>ⓝ</sup> of God.
CSB17.20: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the word by the accompanying signs.]

AMP.1: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome purchased [sweet-smelling] spices, so that they might go and anoint Him.
AMP.2: Very early on the first day of the week, they came to the tomb when the sun had risen.
AMP.3: And they were saying to one another, "Who will roll back the stone for us from the entrance of the tomb?"
AMP.4: Looking up, they saw that the stone had been rolled away, though it was extremely large.
AMP.5: Entering the tomb, they saw a young man sitting on the right, wearing a [long, stately] white robe; and they were amazed and bewildered.
AMP.6: And he said to them, "Do not be amazed; you are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has risen; He is not here. See, [here is] the place where they laid Him.
AMP.7: But go, tell His disciples and Peter, 'He is going ahead of you to Galilee; you will see Him there, just as He told you.'"
AMP.8: They went out and fled from the tomb, for they were seized with trembling and astonishment; and they said nothing [about it] to anyone, because they were afraid.
AMP.9: [Now Jesus, having risen [from death] early on the first day of the week, appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom He had cast out seven demons.
AMP.10: She went and reported it to those who had been with Him, while they were mourning and weeping.
AMP.11: When they heard that He was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe it.
AMP.12: After that, He appeared in a different form to two of them as they were walking along the way to the country.
AMP.13: They returned [to Jerusalem] and told the others, but they did not believe them either.
AMP.14: Later, Jesus appeared to the eleven [disciples] themselves as they were reclining at the table; and He called them to account for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they had not believed those who had seen Him after He had risen [from death].
AMP.15: And He said to them, "Go into all the world and preach the gospel to all creation.
AMP.16: He who has believed [in Me] and has been baptized will be saved [from the penalty of God's wrath and judgment]; but he who has not believed will be condemned.
AMP.17: These signs will accompany those who have believed: in My name they will cast out demons, they will speak in new tongues;
AMP.18: they will pick up serpents, and if they drink anything deadly, it will not hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will get well."
AMP.19: So then, when the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, He was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
AMP.20: And they went out and preached everywhere, while the Lord was working with them and confirming the word by the signs that followed.]

MSG.1: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so they could embalm him.
MSG.2: Very early on Sunday morning, as the sun rose, they went to the tomb.
MSG.3: They worried out loud to each other, "Who will roll back the stone from the tomb for us?"
MSG.4: Then they looked up, saw that it had been rolled back—it was a huge stone—and walked right in.
MSG.5: They saw a young man sitting on the right side, dressed all in white. They were completely taken aback, astonished.
MSG.6: He said, "Don't be afraid. I know you're looking for Jesus the Nazarene, the One they nailed on the cross. He's been raised up; he's here no longer. You can see for yourselves that the place is empty.
MSG.7: Now—on your way. Tell his disciples and Peter that he is going on ahead of you to Galilee. You'll see him there, exactly as he said."
MSG.8: They got out as fast as they could, beside themselves, their heads swimming. Stunned, they said nothing to anyone.
MSG.9: After rising from the dead, Jesus appeared early on Sunday morning to Mary Magdalene, whom he had delivered from seven demons.
MSG.10: She went to his former companions, now weeping and carrying on, and told them.
MSG.11: When they heard her report that she had seen him alive and well, they didn't believe her.
MSG.12: Later he appeared, but in a different form, to two of them out walking in the countryside.
MSG.13: They went back and told the rest, but they weren't believed either.
MSG.14: Still later, as the Eleven were eating supper, he appeared and took them to task most severely for their stubborn unbelief, refusing to believe those who had seen him raised up.
MSG.15: Then he said, "Go into the world. Go everywhere and announce the Message of God's good news to one and all.
MSG.16: Whoever believes and is baptized is saved; whoever refuses to believe is damned.
MSG.17: "These are some of the signs that will accompany believers: They will throw out demons in my name, they will speak in new tongues,
MSG.18: they will take snakes in their hands, they will drink poison and not be hurt, they will lay hands on the sick and make them well."
MSG.19: Then the Master Jesus, after briefing them, was taken up to heaven, and he sat down beside God in the place of honor.
MSG.20: And the disciples went everywhere preaching, the Master working right with them, validating the Message with indisputable evidence.]

NIV.1:
The Resurrection
When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices so that they might go to anoint Jesus’ body.
NIV.2: Very early on the first day of the week, just after sunrise, they were on their way to the tomb
NIV.3: and they asked each other, “Who will roll the stone away from the entrance of the tomb?”
NIV.4: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had been rolled away.
NIV.5: As they entered the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side, and they were alarmed.
NIV.6: “Don’t be alarmed,” he said. “You are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has risen! He is not here. See the place where they laid him.
NIV.7: But go, tell his disciples and Peter, ‘He is going ahead of you into Galilee. There you will see him, just as he told you.’”
NIV.8:
Trembling and bewildered, the women went out and fled from the tomb. They said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid.
[The earliest manuscripts and some other ancient witnesses do not have Mark 16:9–20.]
NIV.9: When Jesus rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, out of whom he had driven seven demons.
NIV.10: She went and told those who had been with him and who were mourning and weeping.
NIV.11: When they heard that Jesus was alive and that she had seen him, they did not believe it.
NIV.12: Afterward Jesus appeared in a different form to two of them while they were walking in the country.
NIV.13: These returned and reported it to the rest; but they did not believe them either.
NIV.14: Later Jesus appeared to the Eleven as they were eating; he rebuked them for their lack of faith and their stubborn refusal to believe those who had seen him after he had risen.
NIV.15: He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the good news to all creation.
NIV.16: Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned.
NIV.17: And these signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
NIV.18: they will pick up snakes with their hands; and when they drink deadly poison, it will not hurt them at all; they will place their hands on sick people, and they will get well.”
NIV.19: After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven and he sat at the right hand of God.
NIV.20: Then the disciples went out and preached everywhere, and the Lord worked with them and confirmed his word by the signs that accompanied it.

NRSVCE.1: When the sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, and Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought spices, so that they might go and anoint him.
NRSVCE.2: And very early on the first day of the week, when the sun had risen, they went to the tomb.
NRSVCE.3: They had been saying to one another, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?”
NRSVCE.4: When they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had already been rolled back.
NRSVCE.5: As they entered the tomb, they saw a young man, dressed in a white robe, sitting on the right side; and they were alarmed.
NRSVCE.6: But he said to them, “Do not be alarmed; you are looking for Jesus of Nazareth, who was crucified. He has been raised; he is not here. Look, there is the place they laid him.
NRSVCE.7: But go, tell his disciples and Peter that he is going ahead of you to Galilee; there you will see him, just as he told you.”
NRSVCE.8: So they went out and fled from the tomb, for terror and amazement had seized them; and they said nothing to anyone, for they were afraid. THE SHORTER ENDING OF MARK [[And all that had been commanded them they told briefly to those around Peter. And afterward Jesus himself sent out through them, from east to west, the sacred and imperishable proclamation of eternal salvation. ]]
NRSVCE.9: [[Now after he rose early on the first day of the week, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had cast out seven demons.
NRSVCE.10: She went out and told those who had been with him, while they were mourning and weeping.
NRSVCE.11: But when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they would not believe it.
NRSVCE.12: After this he appeared in another form to two of them, as they were walking into the country.
NRSVCE.13: And they went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them.
NRSVCE.14: Later he appeared to the eleven themselves as they were sitting at the table; and he upbraided them for their lack of faith and stubbornness, because they had not believed those who saw him after he had risen.
NRSVCE.15: And he said to them, “Go into all the world and proclaim the good news to the whole creation.
NRSVCE.16: The one who believes and is baptized will be saved; but the one who does not believe will be condemned.
NRSVCE.17: And these signs will accompany those who believe: by using my name they will cast out demons; they will speak in new tongues;
NRSVCE.18: they will pick up snakes in their hands, and if they drink any deadly thing, it will not hurt them; they will lay their hands on the sick, and they will recover.”
NRSVCE.19: So then the Lord Jesus, after he had spoken to them, was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
NRSVCE.20: And they went out and proclaimed the good news everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the message by the signs that accompanied it. ]]

NET.1: When the Sabbath was over, Mary Magdalene, Mary the mother of James, and Salome bought aromatic spices so that they might go and anoint him.
NET.2: And very early on the first day of the week, at sunrise, they went to the tomb.
NET.3: They had been asking each other, “Who will roll away the stone for us from the entrance to the tomb?”
NET.4: But when they looked up, they saw that the stone, which was very large, had been rolled back.
NET.5: Then as they went into the tomb, they saw a young man dressed in a white robe sitting on the right side; and they were alarmed.
NET.6: But he said to them, “Do not be alarmed. You are looking for Jesus the Nazarene, who was crucified. He has been raised! He is not here. Look, there is the place where they laid him.
NET.7: But go, tell his disciples, even Peter, that he is going ahead of you into Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.”
NET.8: Then they went out and ran from the tomb, for terror and bewilderment had seized them. And they said nothing to anyone, because they were afraid. [[
NET.9: Early on the first day of the week, after he arose, he appeared first to Mary Magdalene, from whom he had driven out seven demons.
NET.10: She went out and told those who were with him, while they were mourning and weeping.
NET.11: And when they heard that he was alive and had been seen by her, they did not believe.
NET.12: After this he appeared in a different form to two of them while they were on their way to the country.
NET.13: They went back and told the rest, but they did not believe them.
NET.14: Then he appeared to the eleven themselves, while they were eating, and he rebuked them for their unbelief and hardness of heart, because they did not believe those who had seen him resurrected.
NET.15: He said to them, “Go into all the world and preach the gospel to every creature.
NET.16: The one who believes and is baptized will be saved, but the one who does not believe will be condemned.
NET.17: These signs will accompany those who believe: In my name they will drive out demons; they will speak in new languages;
NET.18: they will pick up snakes with their hands, and whatever poison they drink will not harm them; they will place their hands on the sick and they will be well.”
NET.19: After the Lord Jesus had spoken to them, he was taken up into heaven and sat down at the right hand of God.
NET.20: They went out and proclaimed everywhere, while the Lord worked with them and confirmed the word through the accompanying signs.]]
